images.caption: Figure 1:Geometric illustration of ZeroUnlearn. The original sensitive output𝐦f\mathbf{m}_{\textbf{f}}is first projected onto the null space via the projection matrix𝐏\mathbf{P}(Step a). Subsequently, the optimization process aligns the projected representation with the target neutral state𝐦n\mathbf{m}_{n}(Step b) to achieve precise knowledge erasure.

header: 5Few-shot Constraints of ZeroUnlearn and an Alternative Solution

images.caption: Figure 3:PCA visualization of MLP representation shifts at Layer 16 of Llama-3.2 on theMCFdataset.
Figure 4:Evaluation of general capabilities on Llama-3.2.

Figure 5:Component-wise causal tracing analysis on Llama-3.2.The line plots visualize the Average Indirect Effect (AIE) of single hidden vectors (left), MLP lookups (middle), and Attention modules (right) across layers. The results highlight distinct roles: MLP layers in the early stages (layers 0-10) show high causal influence on thelast subject token(green line), suggesting knowledge retrieval, whereas thelast token(red line) dominates the causal effect in later layers, particularly in attention modules.
Figure 6:Average Indirect Effect of Attention modules across different architectures.
